shortDescription.text: Confirms that observed behaviors are legitimate and non-malicious based on external threat intelligence.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies and confirms activities that, while potentially raising initial flags (e.g., shell command execution, file system access), are ultimately determined to be part of legitimate software functionality, especially within the context of AI agent operations or development tools. Verification includes reconciliation with external threat intelligence and a thorough check for malicious intent or indicators.

message.text: The 'completion-gate' skill has been verified as legitimate. Threat intelligence strongly confirmed its role as a quality assurance and workflow enforcement tool for an AI agent. No confirmed malicious intent or activity was found. The observed behaviors, including shell command execution (`ls`, `grep`, `pytest`, `pyright`, `uv run`, `sed`, `wc`) and internal agent memory reading (`mcp__serena__read_memory`), are consistent with documented internal agent mechanisms and standard development practices. No suspicious lines were identified in the initial analysis.
